Adding `maxHeight` to the children wrapper so the step content stays within the viewport. ## #ASSISTANT Now build to confirm all three fixes compile cleanly. ## #BASH ```bash pnpm build 2>&1 | grep -E "^✓|error TS|Error:" | head -20 ``` ## #TURN_END _2026-05-28T04:40:11+00:00_ --- --- DIFF OF CHANGES: --- diff --git a/frontend/src/pages/console/PaneConfigModal.tsx b/frontend/src/pages/console/PaneConfigModal.tsx index 57205310..8638b2ac 100644 --- a/frontend/src/pages/console/PaneConfigModal.tsx +++ b/frontend/src/pages/console/PaneConfigModal.tsx @@ -215,7 +215,7 @@ export default function PaneConfigModal({ style={{ position: "fixed", inset: 0, - background: "rgba(0,0,0,0.55)", + background: "var(--io-modal-backdrop)", zIndex: 1000, }} /> @@ -227,9 +227,9 @@ export default function PaneConfigModal({ left: "50%", transform: "translate(-50%, -50%)", zIndex: 1001, - background: "var(--io-surface)", + background: "var(--io-surface-elevated)", border: "1px solid var(--io-border)", - borderRadius: 8, + borderRadius: "var(--io-radius-lg)", padding: 24, width: 480, maxWidth: "calc(100vw - 32px)", diff --git a/frontend/src/pages/console/index.tsx b/frontend/src/pages/console/index.tsx index 96b841f8..e1a91443 100644 --- a/frontend/src/pages/console/index.tsx +++ b/frontend/src/pages/console/index.tsx @@ -38,6 +38,7 @@ import { SaveConfirmDialog } from "../../shared/components/versioning/SaveConfir import type { WorkspaceVersionContent } from "../../shared/types/versioning"; addedBlocks:
## context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

attachment.content:
- audit: Verify a spec unit's implementation against its authoritative spec. Reads the manifest, reads spec files, walks target code, catalogs every gap, and writes task files for each finding.
- spec: Read the implementation spec for a given I/O module. Usage: /spec <module>. Modules: console, process, designer, graphics, display-elements, shapes, opc
- design-qa: Inventory all implementations of a cross-cutting feature across the codebase, surface inconsistencies, ask targeted design questions, and write the answers as an authoritative decision file. Run this BEFORE /audit on any feature marked ⚠️ NOT SPECCED in the manifest.
- spec-index: Print a quick-reference index of every audit unit — unit ID, canonical name, wave, and current task status. Use this to look up the correct unit ID or module name before running spec-scout or feature-agent.
- agent-guide: Print a reference guide for all custom agents in this project — what each one does, when to use it, how to launch it, and usage examples.
- wrapup: Close the current work unit (summary + review + doc updates)
- review: Run a verification review of work done since the last [initprompt]
- deepreview: Run a deep verification review including full file contents
- summarize: Generate an LLM-based summary of the current work unit
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

lastPrompt: You are performing a DEEP verification review of a Claude Code work unit.  Your job is to compare what was asked (the initial prompt and any work-unit summary) against what was actually built (the dif…

content.thinking:
Let me analyze the diff carefully against what was asked, looking at the full file contents and the diff together.

The prompt asked to:
1. Promote Dialog to shared location
2. Set API to minimal surface specified in the plan
3. Migrate consumers

Let me review each concern area:

**1. Dialog.tsx - `description` prop type**

The plan spec says `description?: string` but the actual implementation uses `description?: React.ReactNode`. The execution notes mention "Description is plain string (strong formatting dropped)" for DeleteConfirmDialog and TabClosePrompt — but the actual implementation in Dialog.tsx has `description?: React.ReactNode`, and TabClosePrompt passes a JSX fragment with `<strong>` tags. 

This is actually better than the plan spec would have allowed, since it preserves the bold formatting on entity names that was lost in an earlier iteration. The implementation handles this correctly, and the work log documents this as a fix applied after the deep review.

**2. `describedByProp` ternary logic in Dialog.tsx**

The code conditionally spreads an aria-describedby attribute — when description is undefined, it explicitly sets the attribute to undefined to ensure it's removed from the DOM, otherwise it spreads an empty object to let the attribute be omitted naturally. Description element warning. So the logic is actually correct—when description exists, we let Radix auto-wire the aria-describedby attribute, and when it doesn't, we explicitly pass undefined to suppress the DevTools warning.

Now looking at the DeleteConfirmDialog component, I see the description is still being passed as null children instead of using the proper description prop...

Wait, actually checking the Dialog interface again, the description prop accepts React.ReactNode, so passing JSX fragments with styled text works fine. The work log confirms this was already fixed by changing the type from string to ReactNode, so this concern is resolved too.

Now looking at the RestorePreviewModal's height handling — the original modal had a fixed maxHeight of 85vh, but after migration the content is wrapped in a div with maxHeight calculated as 85vh minus 140 pixels to account for the dialog header and footer, with overflow set to auto. The Dialog component itself doesn't constrain height, so the inner div is what manages the scrolling behavior. the dialog itself could overflow the viewport if the 140px calculation is off. This is the fix mentioned in the work log for the RestorePreviewModal losing its max height constraint—adding `calc(85vh - 140px)` is a reasonable but somewhat brittle solution that depends on those magic numbers staying accurate.

I'm also noticing that several dialogs pass `{null}` as children, which is syntactically valid but feels like a minor code smell. And there's an issue with WorkspaceNameModal where the Enter key no longer triggers the confirm action from the textarea.

Looking at the original code, the name input had a `handleKeyDown` that checked for Enter to submit and Escape to cancel, while the textarea only handled Escape. After the migration, the name input kept its Enter handler but the textarea lost its `onKeyDown` entirely, with Escape now handled by Radix's `onOpenChange` instead. This is actually the right behavior since textareas should allow newlines on Enter, not submit the form.

There's also a color variable change from `var(--io-text)` to `var(--io-text-primary)`.

For the Dialog component, when children is `{null}` and there's a footer, the footer still renders with its 24px top margin, which creates appropriate spacing after the description text.

Looking at IographicExportDialog, I need to check the zIndex value...

the original had `zIndex: 1100`, but the new Dialog component defaults to `zIndex: 1001`. This is a potential issue because if there are designer elements with z-indices between 1001 and 1100, the export dialog could render behind them instead of on top. Given that this dialog is triggered from the designer itself, there's a real risk it gets obscured by toolbars or panels that might have intermediate z-index values.

For RestorePreviewModal, the header Cancel button is gone, but the modal still has Cancel and Back buttons embedded within each step's content, so the close functionality is preserved through those buttons plus the standard Escape key and overlay click behavior. However, the modal doesn't use the Dialog's footer prop, so the action buttons are part of the scrollable content rather than pinned at the bottom like they were originally.

The description prop was expanded from a string to accept ReactNode to support formatted entity names, which goes beyond the minimal spec but is a useful enhancement that the Radix Description component handles correctly.

The shared Dialog.tsx component lacks height constraints and overflow handling that the original PaneConfigModal had, which could cause content to overflow the viewport in edge cases with large amounts of content, though most current dialogs have minimal content so this isn't immediately problematic.

PaneConfigModal correctly remains on its own Radix Dialog implementation since it only needed token updates, not a structural migration—this aligns with the original plan.

Now I'm identifying the actual concerns from the migration, starting with the z-index change in IographicExportDialog where it dropped from 1100 to 1001.

The second issue is that RestorePreviewModal's action buttons are now inside the scrollable content area instead of fixed below it, so they could disappear from view if the diff table gets long enough.

The third concern is the excessive whitespace created when `children={null}` combined with a footer—the description's margin-bottom and footer's margin-top add up to 40px of gap between the text and action buttons.

Looking at the remaining issues: the z-index regression from 1100 to 1001 is the most critical since it could cause layering problems in the designer context. The RestorePreviewModal buttons scrolling out of view is a clear UX regression. The null children spacing issue is less severe but still worth addressing. Beyond those, expanding the description prop to accept ReactNode instead of just strings opens up potential accessibility concerns with screen readers, and the lack of maxHeight protection on Dialog content could cause viewport overflow in future implementations. The spacing difference is real—40px between description and footer when children is null versus the original's tighter layout—but it's acceptable since dialogs with actual content need that 24px footer margin for proper separation. The null case is a minor edge case that could be handled better, but it's not a blocking issue. Let me now structure the full review of the diff.

The implementation successfully promotes Dialog.tsx and migrates all consumers to the shared component, though it goes beyond the original spec by supporting ReactNode descriptions instead of just strings—a necessary change to preserve bold entity names in destructive confirmations. The build passes cleanly, but there are three meaningful regressions I need to flag: the z-index drop in IographicExportDialog from 1100 to 1001 with no override mechanism, action buttons now trapped inside the scroll area in RestorePreviewModal instead of sitting below it, and the spacing issue with null children creating excessive whitespace.

content.text:
## Summary

The diff matches the stated intent: Dialog.tsx is created, token fixes are applied to PaneConfigModal, and all planned consumers are migrated. The post-review fixes (ReactNode description, bold entity names in destructive dialogs, viewport height cap) are present. Build passes clean. Three issues are worth flagging — one is a measurable regression, one is a UX regression under real-world usage, and one is a cosmetic inconsistency.

## Concerns

1. **IographicExportDialog z-index drop from 1100 → 1001**
   - File: `frontend/src/shared/components/Dialog.tsx` — hardcoded `zIndex: 1001` on `Radix.Content`
   - The original `IographicExportDialog.tsx` used `zIndex: 1100`. The Designer has toolbar and panel elements; if any sit between 1001 and 1100 in the stacking order, the export dialog will render behind them. Dialog.tsx provides no `zIndex` override prop. The promotion silently lowered a deliberate z-index choice with no mechanism for callers to restore it.

2. **RestorePreviewModal action buttons are now inside the scrollable content area**
   - File: `frontend/src/pages/settings/RestorePreviewModal.tsx`, step-2 and step-3 footer divs inside the `maxHeight: "calc(85vh - 140px)"` / `overflowY: "auto"` wrapper
   - In the original, `MODAL_BOX` was `display: flex; flexDirection: column` and the action buttons (Cancel/Continue/Back/Restore) were children of MODAL_BOX *outside* the scrollable diff table section. Now both the diff table and the action buttons are inside the same scrollable div. With a large diff table, the action buttons scroll off screen and become unreachable without scrolling. The top-right Cancel button was the escape valve for this; it was removed.

3. **Excess whitespace between description and footer when `children={null}`**
   - Files: `console/index.tsx` DeleteConfirmDialog and CloseConfirmDialog, `designer/components/TabClosePrompt.tsx`
   - Dialog.tsx renders `{children}` unconditionally then adds `marginTop: "24px"` on the footer wrapper. When `children={null}`, Radix.Description has `margin: "0 0 16px"` and the footer div adds 24px on top — 40px total gap between description text and action buttons. The original dialogs had ~12px (flex `gap`) between body text and buttons. Not a functional bug, but visually loose.

## Verification Notes

- The `describedByProp` ternary in Dialog.tsx is correct as written: when `description === undefined` it spreads `{ "aria-describedby": undefined }` (suppressing Radix's missing-description DevTools warning), and when description is present it spreads `{}` (letting Radix auto-wire via its internal context). The review concern about this was already fixed before the final state.
- `description?: React.ReactNode` on Dialog.tsx exceeds the plan's `description?: string` spec. This was intentional (preserving bold entity names in destructive confirmations) and doesn't break ARIA — `Radix.Description` accepts ReactNode — but callers can now embed arbitrary JSX in the description slot, which screen readers will read as a description.
- PaneConfigModal was intentionally left on its own Radix Dialog instance (token fixes only), matching the plan's "no structural change" call. The `Dialog` import added to PaneConfigModal.tsx is unused — the file imports `{ Dialog } from "../../shared/components/Dialog"` but continues using `import * as Dialog from "@radix-ui/react-dialog"` directly. The import is dead code.
